- Use spell check: Most email clients and word processors have a built-in spell check function. Use it to catch any obvious misspellings before sending your email.
- Watch out for homophones: Homophones are words that sound the same but have different meanings and spellings. Some common ones are "their," "there," and "they're"; "your" and "you're"; and "too," "two," and "to."
- Read it out loud: Reading your email out loud can help you catch any awkward phrasing or missing words.
- Use a grammar checker: There are many online tools that can help you check your grammar and punctuation. While they're not perfect, they can catch some common mistakes.
